schema:name: Frost Day Prediction Random Forest Model
schema:description: A Random Forest Classifier trained on daily meteorological measurements from Vienna Hohe Warte station (2000-2023) to predict frost days, defined as days where the minimum temperature drops below 0°C. The model is part of a FAIR open-science experiment developed for the TU Wien Data Stewardship course 2026.

trainingDataset.schema:name: Messstationen Tagesdaten v2 (Daily Station Measurements)
trainingDataset.schema:description: Daily meteorological measurements from GeoSphere Austria, Vienna Hohe Warte station (ID: 5904), covering 2000-2023.

inputFeatures.name: temp_mean_c
inputFeatures.description: Daily mean air temperature in degrees Celsius

inputFeatures.name: temp_max_c
inputFeatures.description: Daily maximum air temperature in degrees Celsius

inputFeatures.name: temp_min_c
inputFeatures.description: Daily minimum air temperature in degrees Celsius

inputFeatures.name: humidity_pct
inputFeatures.description: Daily mean relative humidity in percent

inputFeatures.name: visibility_m
inputFeatures.description: Daily mean horizontal visibility in metres

targetVariable.name: is_frost_day
targetVariable.description: Binary label: 1 if minimum temperature < 0°C (frost day), 0 otherwise

intendedUse: This model is intended for predicting frost days in Vienna based on daily meteorological measurements. It is suitable for educational and research purposes as a demonstration of FAIR-compliant machine learning workflows. It is not intended for operational meteorological forecasting or safety-critical applications.
outOfScopeUses: The model should not be used for real-time weather forecasting, safety-critical decisions, or predictions for geographic locations other than Vienna Hohe Warte station. The model is trained on historical data from 2000-2023 and may not generalise to future climate conditions.
knownLimitations: The model is trained on data from a single weather station (Vienna Hohe Warte) and may not generalise to other locations.
Missing values in the original dataset were dropped, which may introduce bias.
The model does not account for temporal dependencies between consecutive days.
Climate change may affect the statistical relationships between features and frost days over time.
ethicalConsiderations: The input data is publicly available under a CC0 licence with no privacy concerns. The model predicts a physical phenomenon (frost days) and does not involve personal data or sensitive attributes. No fairness or bias concerns are identified for this use case.

authors.schema:name: Sophie Konecny
authors.role: A - Database schema and model deposit

authors.schema:name: Nayama
authors.role: D - Model training and API reimplementation
